title: Untitled (Wisconsin Dhs Office Of Inspector General)

communications.subject: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Untitled (Wisconsin Dhs Office Of Inspector General)
communications.communication:
April 1, 2026

Records Custodian
Wisconsin Department of Health Services
Office of Inspector General
1 West Wilson Street, Room 950
Madison, WI 53703

Re: Open Records Request Pursuant to Wis. Stat. 19.31-19.39

Dear Records Custodian:

Pursuant to Wisconsin's Open Records Law, Wis. Stat. sections 19.31 through 19.39, I hereby request copies of the following records:

1. All records relating to the complaint I filed with the DHS OIG regarding the handling of the SHARES investigation by Jessica Cooper (Dane County), including any intake records, investigation notes, findings, and disposition documents.

2. All records relating to the oversight review I requested on or about February 20, 2025, regarding Jessica Cooper's closure of the SHARES childcare overpayment investigation despite the DHA IPV finding (FOF-215399, $52,917 concealed income).

3. All communications between DHS OIG investigator Angela Dempewolf and Jessica Cooper regarding the SHARES investigation, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

4. Angela Dempewolf's complete investigative file for the FoodShare/SNAP fraud investigation of Tanya Marie Anderson, including the closure rationale dated on or about February 7, 2025.

5. Any communications between DHS OIG and the USDA Office of Inspector General regarding Tanya Marie Anderson's benefit fraud, from January 1, 2024 through the present.

SCOPE: I am the complaining party who filed the fraud reports and provided supporting documentation (including private investigator memo, forensic accounting, and the DHA IPV decision). I am requesting records relating to my own complaints and the institutional response.

I expect a response as soon as practicable and without delay, as required by Wis. Stat. 19.35(4)(a).

Alexander Liam Lennon
215 S. Century Ave., #227, Waunakee, WI 53597
alennon727@gmail.com | (608) 214-6492

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Untitled (Wisconsin Dhs Office Of Inspector General)
communications.communication:
This email is to acknowledge your request to the Department of Health Services (DHS) Office of the Inspector General (OIG) regarding records on a compliant.  We are in the process of analyzing the request.

OIG gives public records requests high priority and provides requested records as soon as practicable and without delay. A reasonable time for response to a specific public record request depends on the nature of the request, the staff, and other resources available to process the request, the extent of the request, and whether confidential material contained in the responsive records must be redacted.

According to Wis. Stat. § 19.35(3), the Department may charge for locating records if the cost is $50 or more, as well as the costs of copies or scanning and mailing/shipping the records responsive to the request. We will prepare an estimate for the cost of the requested documents, if any, and will submit an invoice to you before compiling the records. Also, under Wis. Stat. § 19.35(3)(f), OIG will require prepayment of the estimated cost of providing a copy of the records to you if the total cost exceeds $5.

You may direct any questions concerning this request to dhsoigopenrecordsrequest@wi.gov<mailto:dhsoigopenrecordsrequest@wi.gov>. 

Sincerely,

Anthony J. Baize
Inspector General
Wisconsin Department of Health Services

NOTICE: This email and any attachments may contain confidential information. Use and further disclosure of the information by the recipient must be consistent with applicable laws, regulations and agreements. If you received this email in error, please notify the sender, delete the email and do not use, disclose or store the information it contains.

communications.subject: RE: Wisconsin Open Records Act Request: Untitled (Wisconsin Dhs Office Of Inspector General)
communications.communication:
This email is to follow up on your open records request to the Department of Health Services (DHS), Office of the Inspector General (OIG) regarding the complaint filed. The responsive records are attached.

Include this if redacted:
Personal identifying information has been redacted pursuant to Wis. Stat. § 19.36(6) (6) SEPARATION OF INFORMATION. If a record contains information that is subject to disclosure under s. 19.35 (1) (a) or (am) and information that is not subject to such disclosure, the authority having custody of the record shall provide the information that is subject to disclosure and delete the information that is not subject to disclosure from the record before release.


  *   MA recipient names and dates of birth have been redacted pursuant to 42 USC §1396a(a)(7), 42 C.F.R. s. 431.300 et seq., and Wis. Admin. Code s. DHS 108.01(1) & (4).

If you believe that the Department has improperly determined that materials should not be provided to you, please do not hesitate to contact me. Further, if you do believe, you have a right under Wis. Stat. § 19.37(1) to have the determination reviewed by mandamus or upon application to the attorney general or appropriate district attorney.

This fulfills your open records request, and we will consider your request closed. If you should have any questions, you may contact me at dhsoigopenrecordsrequest@wi.gov<mailto:dhsoigopenrecordsrequest@wi.gov>. 

Sincerely,
 
Anthony J. Baize
Inspector General
Wisconsin Department of Health Services
 
NOTICE: This email and any attachments may contain confidential information. Use and further disclosure of the information by the recipient must be consistent with applicable laws, regulations and agreements. If you received this email in error, please notify the sender, delete the email and do not use, disclose or store the information it contains.

title: Carville Ave 2020 Call Reports/Logs

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Carville Ave 2020 Call Reports/Logs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Could I please obtain a copy of any call logs or reports from calls on Carville Ave throughout the year of 2020.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett

title: Health and mental healthcare and juvenile justice (Louisiana Office Of Juvenile Justice)

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Health and mental healthcare and juvenile justice (Louisiana Office Of Juvenile Justice)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern,

This is a request for records under your state public records law.  I am requesting copies of public records regarding health and behavioural health service delivery in youth correctional facilities in your jurisdiction, to inform a comparative research project regarding health and mental health care delivery in juvenile detention facilities across the US. Specifically, I request information regarding the following:
1. Name and contact details for those responsible for health and behavioural health services provisions at all locations where juveniles are incarcerated within your jurisdiction.
2. Regulations, policies and procedures that govern the operation of health and behavioral health provision for juveniles incarcerated within your jurisdiction. This includes health intake, room confinement, medication management,  access to specialist and dental services, discharge planning services, and other health and behavioral health related policies.
3. Contracts from January 1, 2021 to the time the request is filled between your agency and any healthcare provider including both physical health and behavioral health. 
4. Records concerning your jurisdiction’s most recent application to Centre for Medicaid Services (CMS) for an 1115 Medicaid waiver (if applicable), and any response, with specifically focus on how and whether such a waiver applies to provision of Medicaid funded services for detained juveniles.
5. Records relating to financing for health and behavioural health care delivery in your jurisdiction. 

I request the records be provided electronically. If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of the administration of juvenile justice. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes.

If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than allowed by statute, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies of the requested records.

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

Thank you for considering my request.

Sincerely,

Tess Kelly 
Fellow, Harvard FXB Centre for Health and Human Rights 

Joshua Dankoff
Director of Strategic Initiatives
Citizens for Juvenile Justice
617.338.1050

This is a request for records under your state public records law. I am requesting copies of public records regarding health and behavioural health service delivery in youth correctional facilities in your jurisdiction, to inform a comparative research project regarding health and mental health care delivery in juvenile detention facilities across the US. Specifically, I request information regarding the following:
1. Name and contact details for those responsible for health and behavioural health services provisions at all locations where juveniles are incarcerated within your jurisdiction.
2. Regulations, policies and procedures that govern the operation of health and behavioral health provision for juveniles incarcerated within your jurisdiction. This includes health intake, room confinement, medication management, access to specialist and dental services, discharge planning services, and other health and behavioral health related policies.
3. Contracts from January 1, 2021 to the time the request is filled between your agency and any healthcare provider including both physical health and behavioral health.
4. Records concerning your jurisdiction’s most recent application to Centre for Medicaid Services (CMS) for an 1115 Medicaid waiver (if applicable), and any response, with specifically focus on how and whether such a waiver applies to provision of Medicaid funded services for detained juveniles.
5. Records relating to financing for health and behavioural health care delivery in your jurisdiction.

I request the records be provided electronically. If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of the administration of juvenile justice. title: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Alabama A&M Unversity)

communications.subject: Alabama Open Records Law Request: Annual NCAA MFRS FY25 Report (Alabama A&M Unversity)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Alabama Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Greetings,

I am looking to make a request for records related to your athletic department.

1) I would like a copy of the annual NCAA MFRS report for FY2017 - FY2025. These annual reports are submitted by the athletic department and record the revenue/expenses of the department. It is an approximately 90 page document listing revenue and expenses in the athletic department by team. It is a standard annual NCAA form. The latest FY2025 report was due, 1/15/2025 so should be available. If you are unable to include this, can you please specify if you will send it to me immediately when it's available or if you want me to make a new request. That should be 9 reports combined.

2) I'd like copies of the latest contracts for your athletic director and head coaches of football, mens/womens basketball, and mens/womens ice hockey if you have those teams. That should be 6 documents if you have that many teams.

Please let me know if you have any questions. Thank you for your time.

Best,

Greg Chick

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

André Natta, a citizen of Alabama, in coordination with Greg Chick.

title: Guides, policies and trainings on electronic federal records (U.S. Department Of Agriculture)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Guides, policies and trainings on electronic federal records (U.S. Department Of Agriculture)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

- All guides, memos or briefings on storing and handling electronic records, including but not limited to text messages, as federal records. 

- All polices on storing and handling electronic records, including but not limited to text messages, as federal records. 

-  All employee training materials on storing and handling electronic records, including but not limited to text messages, as federal records.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

communications.subject: USDA FOIA Request No. 2026-OGC-02253-F - Final Response
communications.communication:
Good afternoon, Dillon Bergin.

Please find attached the final response letter and corresponding responsive records associated with a Freedom of Information Act request you submitted to the U.S. Department of Agriculture, Office of Information Affairs (OIA). These records and the information linked in the letter respond to all records management-related requests you submitted to USDA.

Have a nice weekend!

Best,
Christine Jordan, Esq.
FOIA & RM Contracting Program Mgr.
Office of Information Affairs (OIA)

[cid:image001.png@01DCE48E.EF78C2B0]
Contractor for the Office of the General Counsel (OGC)
1400 Independence Avenue, S.W., Washington, D.C., 20250
c: (703) 675-6943





This electronic message contains information generated by the USDA solely for the intended recipients. Any unauthorized interception of this message or the use or disclosure of the information it contains may violate the law and subject the violator to civil or criminal penalties. If you believe you have received this message in error, please notify the sender and delete the email immediately.

title: 2026 Brady List (Kittitas County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Kittitas County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R011308-020326
communications.communication:
Dear Rose Terse: 
 The Kittitas County Prosecuting Attorney's Office received a public records request from you on February 03, 2026. You requested: "To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2208088/" A Public Records Officer of the Prosecuting Attorney's Office will be helping you with your records request. An initial search and review of records matching your request will be completed and reply will be posted within five (5) business days.  This reply will either provide the requested records, ask you for clarification of your request, provide an estimate of fees, or, if your request is broad and involves a large number of records, you’ll be provided a schedule of response installments. Please note, the State of Washington Public Records Act, Chapter 42.56 RCW, does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. 
You can monitor the progress of your request in the Kittitas County Public Records Portal.  For your convenience, we are providing links to the legal authority which determines our response to public records requests: 
 
  *  Public Records Act, Chapter RCW 42.56 Kittitas County Codes Chapter 2.55 Thank you for your interest in public records. Sincerely, Prosecuting Attorney's Office 
Kittitas County, WA

communications.subject: [Records Center] Public Records Request :: R011308-020326
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
  
    Kittitas County Prosecuting Attorney's Office RE: PUBLIC RECORDS REQUEST of February 03, 2026, Reference # R011308-020326 Dear Rose Terse, The Prosecuting Attorney's Office received a public records request from you on February 03, 2026. You requested: 
"To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2208088/" 
As the Public Records Officer for the Prosecuting Attorney's Office, I will be helping you with your records request. Our procedure is to locate records, review them for exemptions, and then prepare them for your inspection and/or copying. After careful consideration of your request and our current workload, we reasonably believe that the records (if any) responsive to your request will be available on or before 2/20/2026. If the records become available in advance of this estimate, I will contact you promptly. Conversely, if we encounter a need to extend our estimate, I will contact you with a revised estimated date. Thank you for your patience in awaiting this information. If you have any questions or need additional information, please feel free to reply to this email or call our office at 509-962-7520. Sincerely, Christine Rice 
Public Records Officer 
Prosecuting Attorney's Office

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

title: Texas peace officer license and disciplinary records for Sgt. Kelly Sargent

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Documents showing the current and historical license status of Kelly Sargent (sometimes listed as “Kelly E. Sargent”), including peace officer license number, issuance date, current status, and any prior changes in status (such as inactive, suspended, revoked, surrendered, or canceled).
Any final disciplinary actions taken by TCOLE regarding Kelly Sargent’s peace officer license, including but not limited to notices of suspension, revocation, surrender, or other decertification; final orders; and any associated findings of fact or conclusions of law.
Any F‑5 or equivalent separation reports, and any reports or notifications submitted to TCOLE by the University of North Texas Police Department concerning the separation or termination of Kelly Sargent, and any TCOLE responses or notices to the agency regarding that separation.
Any records, reports, or notations in Kelly Sargent’s TCOLE licensing file that indicate he is ineligible for reappointment or re-licensure, or that reflect a bar, restriction, or flag on his peace officer license.
If any of these records are available via TCOLE’s Public License Lookup or other online system, please identify the relevant records and provide any additional responsive materials that are not available to the public online.
I request that you produce all reasonably segregable non‑exempt portions of responsive records and provide a written explanation citing the specific sections of Texas law relied upon to withhold or redact any information. If possible, I prefer electronic production via upload or email.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Drew Bee

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R002134-041226
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: Public Records Request of April 12, 2026, Reference # R002134-041226 Good afternoon, I apologize for the extended turnaround time. The PIA role has undergone some personnel changes and I will be taking over the open records requests. Please login to the portal to view the Personal Status Report (PSR)s in reference to Kelly E Sargent. The Personal Status Report (PSR) is a cumulative document of submitted TCOLE forms (L-1s, F-5s, F-7, F-8, certification applications, reactivation applications, reinstatement application, training rosters, academy completions, etc.) as such the PSR shows employment history, training history, certifications awarded, licenses awarded, college credit, military service credit, license disciplinary actions, and license status. Review of TCOLE records found no disciplinary actions taken by TCOLE. Please note that TCOLE does not manage disciplinary records at a local level. For further assistance, reach out to the employing law enforcement agency(s)’s internal affairs department(s). For questions or additional information, please reply to this email. If you are looking for information regarding a Texas law enforcement licensee’s employment, license status, or training courses, please use TCOLE's Public License Lookup (PLL) database. Go to TCOLE's website under Online Services or click the link below to create a login and begin your search. License Lookup 
For questions or additional information, please reply to this email. Sincerely, Elisabeth Rodriguez 
Government and External Relations

title: Disciplinary and separation records for Sgt. Kelly Sargent – UNT Police Department

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Disciplinary and separation records for Sgt. Kelly Sargent – UNT Police Department
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records relating to any internal affairs investigations, administrative investigations, or misconduct investigations involving Sgt. Kelly Sargent (sometimes listed as "Sergeant Kelly E. Sargent",  "Sgt. Sargent" and "Sgt. Kelly Sargent (S‑22)") of the University of North Texas Police Department, from January 1, 2016 to December 31, 2018. This includes, but is not limited to, complaints, investigative reports, witness statements, findings, recommendations, disciplinary letters, notices of suspension, and notices of termination.
All records showing any disciplinary action, including written reprimands, suspensions, demotions, or termination, taken against Sgt. Kelly Sargent during the same period.
Records reflecting any change in employment status for Sgt. Kelly Sargent during the same period, including but not limited to resignation letters, separation notices, last day of employment, settlement or separation agreements, and any documents stating the reasons for his separation from employment.
Any correspondence (emails or letters) between UNT Police Department command staff or Human Resources and Sgt. Kelly Sargent that discusses discipline, performance issues, or separation from employment during the same period, to the extent such records are not exempt from disclosure.
In addition, please provide any records, including but not limited to F‑5 separation reports, notices to or from the Texas Commission on Law Enforcement (TCOLE), and any correspondence with TCOLE, reflecting whether Sgt. Kelly Sargent’s Texas peace officer license was ever suspended, revoked, surrendered, or otherwise decertified, and the reasons for any such action.
If you contend that any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, please produce all reasonably segregable non‑exempt portions and provide a written explanation citing the specific sections of Texas law you rely on to withhold or redact any material.
I am not requesting Social Security numbers, home addresses, dates of birth, or other information that is specifically exempt by law, and I agree that such personal identifiers may be redacted.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Drew Bee

title: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of any local ordinances/bylaws (under RSA 31:39) and other policies regulating conflicts of interest, financial disclosures, reporting requirements and incompatibility standards for municipial officers and employees.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

title: NDOW Big Game Poaching Incidents (Aug 2025–Dec 2026)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: NDOW Big Game Poaching Incidents (Aug 2025–Dec 2026)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am seeking records from the Nevada Department of Wildlife regarding reported big game poaching incidents occurring between August 2025 and December 2025. This request specifically includes all incident reports, citations, investigations, and any related documentation associated with these cases.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Madison Burrows

title: Christman American Petroleum Institute 1

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Christman American Petroleum Institute 1
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all written or electronic communications, including text messages, Signal messages, emails and attachments, between Senior Advisor Jordan Christman and any staff member, lobbyist, employee, director or agent of the American Petroleum Institute.

This request seeks records produced since April 1, 2025. 

I am a reporter for Public Domain and seek a fee waiver. If that waiver is denied i am willing to pay $25.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jimmy Tobias

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: The United States Department of Transportation, Washington DC
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any investigation of double dipping, forgery, or fraud of the McLennan County Sheriff's department. All memos, emails, letters, reports, or any communications concerning any allegations of double dipping by McLennan County Sheriff's officers, Nixon, Eubank, Eubank, Wilcock, Prevynovak, and others beginning in 2021 until the present. This could be out of your Ft.Worth office and REginald Lee.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bernadette Feazell

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #OIG-2022-0079
communications.communication:
I, with informants inside and outside of the McLennan County Sheriff's department, under the Public Information Act, got copies of the Sheriff's deputies time sheets, and compared these sheets to the sheets obtained through the Texas Department of Transportation,  we also sent them to the United States Dept. of Transportation and I received a call from Reginald Lee of the Ft. Worth Office. My group and I put ourselves at risk and proved that indeed the McLennan County Sheriff deputies, about twelve of them, were double dipping, it was obvious in the comparison of the documents.

Both Reginald Lee and Holly  Zakarias talked to me, thanked us for our work, and since I obtained these records through MUCKROCK, they're online and publc forever.

Informants called Reginald Lee and we all sat around and waited. We waited and waited. We're still waiting.

Something happened and this entire thing got closed down and forgotten. WHAM. None of us can even get in touch with Reginald Lee or Holly Zakarias. 

We are in danger and this is a travesty, please do something.
B

THIS is a terrible thing to do to people who still care about corruption, especially in Texas where the crooks run free over the rest of us and you just let them do it. Unreal. This is so shameful. Guess nothing matters at all anymore. Thanks for tipping me over giving up or not. I give up, the crooks and those who let this go on, you, have won.

On Feb. 17, 2023:
Subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #OIG-2022-0079
I, with informants inside and outside of the McLennan County Sheriff's department, under the Public Information Act, got copies of the Sheriff's deputies time sheets, and compared these sheets to the sheets obtained through the Texas Department of Transportation, we also sent them to the United States Dept. of Transportation and I received a call from Reginald Lee of the Ft. Worth Office. My group and I put ourselves at risk and proved that indeed the McLennan County Sheriff deputies, about twelve of them, were double dipping, it was obvious in the comparison of the documents.

Both Reginald Lee and Holly Zakarias talked to me, thanked us for our work, and since I obtained these records through MUCKROCK, they're online and publc forever.

Informants called Reginald Lee and we all sat around and waited. We waited and waited. We're still waiting.

Something happened and this entire thing got closed down and forgotten. WHAM. None of us can even get in touch with Reginald Lee or Holly Zakarias.

We are in danger and this is a travesty, please do something.
B

---

On Aug. 25, 2022:
Subject: RE: {From External} RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: The United States Department of Transportation, Washington DC
Dear Bernadette Feazell:

U.S. Department of Transportation (USDOT), Office of the Inspector General (OIG) has processed, referred, and closed your request below. We sent you the attached response letter on June 30, 2022. Please see the attached correspondence in response to your DOT OIG FOIA Request, Control No.: OIG-2022-0079 and confirm the receipt.

Very Respectfully,

[2021 DOTOIG Logo Master 300dpi-01]Tonya Austin-Peartree
Government Information Specialist
Office of the Chief Counsel
USDOT | Office of Inspector General
tonya.austin-peartree@oig.dot.gov<mailto:tonya.austin-peartree@oig.dot.gov>
www.oig.dot.gov
---

On June 27, 2022:
Subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: The United States Department of Transportation, Washington DC
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any investigation of double dipping, forgery, or fraud of the McLennan County Sheriff's department. All memos, emails, letters, reports, or any communications concerning any allegations of double dipping by McLennan County Sheriff's officers, Nixon, Eubank, Eubank, Wilcock, Prevynovak, and others beginning in 2021 until the present. This could be out of your Ft.Worth office and REginald Lee.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Bernadette Feazell

title: ShotSpotter Contract (Miami Police Department)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: ShotSpotter Contract (Miami Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the current contract between this department, the city, and ShotSpotter, Inc. This request includes the full ShotSpotter Services Agreement, Standard Terms and Conditions, terms of service (including square miles covered, total cost, and contract beginning and end dates), any reference service level agreements, contract amendments, cure notices, non-disclosure agreements, or other revisions to agreements.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Katie Ryan

communications.subject: Miami Florida Police Open Records Center :: W032965-072522
communications.communication:
The Miami Police Department acknowledges the below public records request dated 7/25/2022. The City of Miami will make a reasonable effort to determine from other officers or employees within the City whether such records exist and, if so, the location at which the record can be accessed or copied or if any exemptions apply. You will be notified accordingly. 

You can monitor the progress of your request under "My Requests". Again, thank you for using the Open Records Center.

communications.communication:
Hi there! 

Thanks for providing the renewal document. Can you please also send the ShotSpotter Respond Services Agreement (formerly known as "ShotSpotter Flex") and the terms of service (including square miles covered, total cost since Miami started using ShotSpotter, and the contract "go live" date)? These were part of the original request. 

Grateful for your help.

communications.subject: Fw: Florida Sunshine Law Request #W032965-072522
communications.communication:
Greetings Katie.
The ShotSpotter agreement you requested on July 25, 2022, was emailed to you through the GovQA platform on November 17, 2022.

If you require any additional information, please direct future correspondence to the PIO email address rather than the SMU email account.

It is my understanding that the MuckRock platform sends automated follow-up emails requesting updates on public records requests until the requester manually closes or completes the request. If that is the case, please disable the automatic update function for this request to avoid unnecessary back-and-forth communications.

In the event you did not receive the “ShotSpotter Respond Services Agreement” (formerly known as “ShotSpotter Flex”), along with the associated terms of service — including the square mileage covered, the total cost incurred since Miami began using ShotSpotter, and the contract’s “go-live” date — as part of the original public records request you referenced, I will reopen the request and include this notation.



Thank you.


Orlando Rodriguez
Public Relations Agent
Public Information Office
City of Miami Police Department
400 N.W. 2nd Ave, Miami, FL 33128
[cid:c68558aa-b9ef-4e75-8b26-6cb7ee296094]

title: 2026 Brady List (Adams County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Adams County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse
Attached is the PID you requested. This will complete your PRA request.

communications.subject: RE: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Adams County Prosecuting Attorney)

Hello,

Thank you for your quick response. As mentioned in my original request, would you please also provide the accompanying impeachment materials for each officer on the list?

Thank you,
Rose

title: Kevin Riford Reports Niagara County Bail Bond Fraud to NYS Department of Financial Services - E-mail Communications & More (Attempt #2)

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Kevin Riford Reports Niagara County Bail Bond Fraud to NYS Department of Financial Services - E-mail Communications & More (Attempt #2)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

On 01/08/2025 I submitted a request for the records below. My request was denied citing "The records requested are records of a current Department investigation, and thus are exempt from disclosure pursuant to N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(e)(i), which excepts from disclosure records that are compiled for law enforcement purposes and that, if disclosed, would interfere with law enforcement investigations" as the reason for denial. SEE EXHIBIT A

The investigation is now closed (see attached exhibit b page 18), as such I am resubmitting my original request for email communications in addition to other requests for documents. Please provide the following:

1.)  Any and all emails and attachments received by william.fedrau@dfs.ny.gov, laura.sankey@dfs.ny.gov, warren.lester@dfs.ny.gov from kevinriford1@protonmail.com for the following time period 01/01/2023 through 01/23/2025

2.) Any and all emails and attachments sent from william.fedrau@dfs.ny.gov, laura.sankey@dfs.ny.gov, warren.lester@dfs.ny.gov to kevinriford1@protonmail to for the following time period 01/01/2023 through 01/23/2025. I have attached copies of the referenced email communications for clarity. SEE EXHIBIT B

3.) A copy of the email and letter attached as EXHIBIT C - which was sent to Niagara County DA Brian Seaman, Erie County DA Michael Keane and others.

4.) All investigative records for Dept. of Financial services case #, CSB-2023-01537055, including the below: 

Complaint intake forms, notes, or logs relating to CSB-2023-01537055 submissions.

Case file summary or investigative reports prepared by DFS staff related to CSB-2023-01537055

Interview notes, transcripts, or recordings related to CSB-2023-01537055

Copies of subpoenas issued and responses received related to CSB-2023-01537055

Copies of correspondence sent or received in connection with the investigation (letters, memos, emails not already covered) related to CSB-2023-01537055

Exhibits, attachments, and supporting documents collected during the investigation related to CSB-2023-01537055

Internal memoranda, briefing notes, or recommendations concerning the matter related to CSB-2023-01537055

Final investigative findings, closure memo, or disposition documents related to CSB-2023-01537055

Administrative Records related to CSB-2023-01537055

Logs of record requests, case tracking notes, or audit trail entries related to CSB-2023-01537055

Any inter-agency referrals or communications with the Niagara County DA, Erie County DA, or other law enforcement agencies related to CSB-2023-01537055

Any determination letters or reports sent to complainants or other stakeholders related to CSB-2023-01537055

Pursuant to N.Y. Public Officers Law §§ 87(1)(b)(iii) and 89(3), I request that all records produced in response to this request be provided as certified copies, attesting that they are true and accurate copies of records maintained by the Department of Financial Services.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

The Riford Family

title: Open Records Request: Flock audits

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Request: Flock audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules (Rochester Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules (Rochester Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of any local ordinances/bylaws (under RSA 31:39) and other policies regulating conflicts of interest, financial disclosures, reporting requirements and incompatibility standards for municipial officers and employees.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: RE: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Ethics and Conflict of Interest Rules (Rochester Clerk)
communications.communication:
The portion of our Charter related to Conflicts of Interest can be found online: https://ecode360.com/32212208
Incompatibility standards are also available within our online ordinances: https://ecode360.com/32216407 and https://ecode360.com/32216408

title: Alleged Unlawful Sensitive/Classified Technology Transfer at CalTech/JPL

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Alleged Unlawful Sensitive/Classified Technology Transfer at CalTech/JPL
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records pertaining to alleged espionage and/or violation(s) of the Arms Export Control Act and/or International Traffic in Arms Regulations and related statutes at the California Institute of Technology and/or the Jet Propulsion Laboratory between 2010 and 2017, inclusive. The records I am requesting include, but are not limited to, those pertaining to alleged criminal conduct pertaining to the Electrospray Thruster Array Technology Feasibility Study, which was funded, in part, by the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency.

This request is for any and all responsive records at all relevant department/agency offices and facilities including, but not limited to, headquarters.

In order to facilitate the location of the requested records I am providing the following list of names that may be associated with these allegations and any related Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) inquiry: Amir Gat, Sandra Troian, Daniel Weihs, Morteza Gharib, Kelly M. Sullivan, and, David Tsang.

Upon information and belief, Kelly M. Sullivan and David Tsang were FBI agents with the Los Angeles County Counterintelligence Division at the times relevant to this request. The personal names provided above should not be construed as a basis for limiting the scope of this records request.

In order to help to determine my status to assess fees, you should know that I am a representative of the news media affiliated with the Randolph Bourne Institute and its media outlet, Antiwar.com. This request is made as part of news gathering and not for a commercial use.

I request a waiver of all fees for this request. Disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations or activities of the government and is not primarily in my commercial interest. Specifically, this information will help advance public understanding of foreign intelligence activities targeted against the United States of America.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michelle Kinnucan

communications.subject: Request Acknowledgement by NASA Public Access Link
communications.communication:
Dear Michelle  Kinnucan, 

Case Number 26-00553-F-OIG was assigned to the request you submitted. You seek the following: 

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records pertaining to alleged espionage and/or violation(s) of the Arms Export Control Act and/or International Traffic in Arms Regulations and related statutes at the California Institute of Technology and/or the Jet Propulsion Laboratory between 2010 and 2017, inclusive. The records I am requesting include, but are not limited to, those pertaining to alleged criminal conduct pertaining to the Electrospray Thruster Array Technology Feasibility Study, which was funded, in part, by the Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency.

This request is for any and all responsive records at all relevant department/agency offices and facilities including, but not limited to, headquarters.

In order to facilitate the location of the requested records I am providing the following list of names that may be associated with these allegations and any related Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) inquiry: Amir Gat, Sandra Troian, Daniel Weihs, Morteza Gharib, Kelly M. Sullivan, and, David Tsang.

Upon information and belief, Kelly M. Sullivan and David Tsang were FBI agents with the Los Angeles County Counterintelligence Division at the times relevant to this request. The personal names provided above should not be construed as a basis for limiting the scope of this records request.

In order to help to determine my status to assess fees, you should know that I am a representative of the news media affiliated with the Randolph Bourne Institute and its media outlet, Antiwar.com. This request is made as part of news gathering and not for a commercial use.

I request a waiver of all fees for this request. Disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations or activities of the government and is not primarily in my commercial interest. Specifically, this information will help advance public understanding of foreign intelligence activities targeted against the United States of America.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michelle Kinnucan

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2217141/ 

Please reference case number 26-00553-F-OIG in all future correspondence regarding this reequest.
Regards, 
NASA Public Access Link

title: Public Records Request: Flock audits (South Hadley Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Public Records Request: Flock audits (South Hadley Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

title: Ohio Highway Shootings

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Ohio Highway Shootings
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting public records related to incidents of shootings and violence occurring on roadways under the jurisdiction of the Ohio State Highway Patrol (OSHP), including the Ohio Turnpike and Interstate Highway System. The date range for these records is from January 1, 2023, to December 31, 2025.

This request is divided into three parts: records identified by specific criminal statutes, records identified by a keyword search of narratives, and any existing compiled data.

PART 1: RECORDS BY STATUTE

Please provide all Incident Reports (OH-1 or OH-2 forms if applicable in this context) and Case Reports for cases where the Ohio State Highway Patrol was the primary investigating agency and charges were filed (or the incident was classified) under the following Ohio Revised Code (ORC) statutes.

Please limit results to incidents occurring on state highways, the Ohio Turnpike, or interstates.

HOMICIDE & ATTEMPTED HOMICIDE:
-- ORC § 2903.01 (Aggravated Murder)
-- ORC § 2903.02 (Murder)
-- ORC § 2923.02 / § 2903.02 (Attempted Murder)

ASSAULT, BATTERY & DISCHARGE OF FIREARMS:
-- ORC § 2903.11 (Felonious Assault - specifically (A)(2) Causing or attempting to cause physical harm to another by means of a deadly weapon or dangerous ordnance)
-- ORC § 2903.12 (Aggravated Assault - specifically (A)(2) involving a deadly weapon)
-- ORC § 2923.16 (Improperly handling firearms in a motor vehicle - specifically (A) Discharging a firearm while in or on a motor vehicle)

ROADWAY & VEHICULAR ENDANGERMENT:
-- ORC § 2903.08 (Aggravated vehicular assault)
-- ORC § 2917.11 (Disorderly Conduct - specifically if flagged with weapons modifiers)
-- ORC § 2903.21 (Aggravated Menacing - specifically involving brandishing of firearms in road rage incidents)

####

PART 2: RECORDS BY KEYWORD SEARCH

Please perform a search of the narratives (Investigative Notes/Remarks) within offense and incident reports for incidents that include any of the following keywords and phrases: ("Road rage" OR "Traffic dispute" OR "Aggressive driving" OR "Reckless driving") AND ("Firearm" OR "gun" OR "shots fired" OR "shot fired" OR "brandishing" OR "gun pointed" OR "pointed gun")

####

PART 3: EXISTING DATA & LOGS

Please provide any existing reports, spreadsheets, or databases compiled by OSHP that track shooting incidents, road rage, or felonious assaults on Ohio highways for the years 2023–2025.

####

FORMAT OF RECORDS REQUESTED:
-- Summary Data: If possible, please provide a summary of all responsive cases in a structured data format (e.g., Excel or CSV) containing the case/incident number, date, time, location (county, highway/route, and nearest mile marker/exit), and all offenses charged.
-- Complete Reports: For each responsive case, please provide the complete initial Incident Report and any narrative supplements.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Chip Brownlee

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R044127-020526
communications.communication:
Dear Chip Brownlee: 
Thank you for your interest in obtaining public records from the Ohio Department of Public Safety.  Your request has been received and is being processed in accordance with the Ohio Revised Code Chapter 149.  Your request was received in this office on 2/5/2026 and given the reference number R044127-020526 for tracking purposes. Records Requested: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

I am requesting public records related to incidents of shootings and violence occurring on roadways under the jurisdiction of the Ohio State Highway Patrol (OSHP), including the Ohio Turnpike and Interstate Highway System. The date range for these records is from January 1, 2023, to December 31, 2025. 

This request is divided into three parts: records identified by specific criminal statutes, records identified by a keyword search of narratives, and any existing compiled data. 

PART 1: RECORDS BY STATUTE 

Please provide all Incident Reports (OH-1 or OH-2 forms if applicable in this context) and Case Reports for cases where the Ohio State Highway Patrol was the primary investigating agency and charges were filed (or the incident was classified) under the following Ohio Revised Code (ORC) statutes. 

Please limit results to incidents occurring on state highways, the Ohio Turnpike, or interstates. 

HOMICIDE & ATTEMPTED HOMICIDE: 
-- ORC § 2903.01 (Aggravated Murder) 
-- ORC § 2903.02 (Murder) 
-- ORC § 2923.02 / § 2903.02 (Attempted Murder) 

ASSAULT, BATTERY & DISCHARGE OF FIREARMS: 
-- ORC § 2903.11 (Felonious Assault - specifically (A)(2) Causing or attempting to cause physical harm to another by means of a deadly weapon or dangerous ordnance) 
-- ORC § 2903.12 (Aggravated Assault - specifically (A)(2) involving a deadly weapon) 
-- ORC § 2923.16 (Improperly handling firearms in a motor vehicle - specifically (A) Discharging a firearm while in or on a motor vehicle) 

ROADWAY & VEHICULAR ENDANGERMENT: 
-- ORC § 2903.08 (Aggravated vehicular assault) 
-- ORC § 2917.11 (Disorderly Conduct - specifically if flagged with weapons modifiers) 
-- ORC § 2903.21 (Aggravated Menacing - specifically involving brandishing of firearms in road rage incidents) 

#### 

PART 2: RECORDS BY KEYWORD SEARCH 

Please perform a search of the narratives (Investigative Notes/Remarks) within offense and incident reports for incidents that include any of the following keywords and phrases: ("Road rage" OR "Traffic dispute" OR "Aggressive driving" OR "Reckless driving") AND ("Firearm" OR "gun" OR "shots fired" OR "shot fired" OR "brandishing" OR "gun pointed" OR "pointed gun") 

#### 

PART 3: EXISTING DATA & LOGS 

Please provide any existing reports, spreadsheets, or databases compiled by OSHP that track shooting incidents, road rage, or felonious assaults on Ohio highways for the years 2023–2025. 

#### 

FORMAT OF RECORDS REQUESTED: 
-- Summary Data: If possible, please provide a summary of all responsive cases in a structured data format (e.g., Excel or CSV) containing the case/incident number, date, time, location (county, highway/route, and nearest mile marker/exit), and all offenses charged. 
-- Complete Reports: For each responsive case, please provide the complete initial Incident Report and any narrative supplements. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days. 

Sincerely, 

Chip Brownlee 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2210846/ 
Your request will be sent to the relevant department(s) to locate the records you seek. Requests are processed in the order in which they are received; please do not submit duplicate entries for the same information. You will be contacted about the availability and/or provided with copies of the records in question based on your selected preferred method of delivery. PLEASE NOTE: The Ohio Public Records Act codified in R.C. Chapter 149 does not require a governmental body to create new information or records that do not already exist or are beyond state records retention schedules. 
 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you will be contacted when your request has been completed. 
 
 
Again, thank you for using the Ohio Department of Public Safety - Public Records Center. Effective July 1, 2025, the Ohio State Highway Patrol will assess a fee of $38.90 per hour of video requested. This fee is the cost associated with reviewing, blurring, redacting, uploading, and producing the video records (R.C. 149.43(B)(1)). Once we receive the requested video recordings from the post, if any, an invoice shall be provided to you with the associated costs and must be paid prior to processing the videos. If an invoice is not paid within 30 days, the invoice will be cancelled and the request for videos will be closed. All other items contained in the request will still be processed and released in accordance with Ohio Public Records law regardless of whether an invoice is paid. For example, a requester who asks for body cam video, a case report, and a training record but does not pay the video invoice within 30 days will still receive the case report and training record but the video will not be processed and released. 
Ohio Department of Public Safety

title: The Zebra/Death Angels Files (San Francisco Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: The Zebra/Death Angels Files (San Francisco Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any extant files regarding the 1973-74 Zebra Killings, including, but not limited to, case files, audio recordings, transcripts, and inter-office memoranda regarding the arrest and conviction of four men responsible for the murder and attempted murder of nearly two dozen San Franciscans from October 1973 - April 1974.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

communications.subject: San Francisco Police Public Records Request :: P221770-090125
communications.communication:
Dear Andrew Free: 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of the San Francisco Police Department ("SFPD"). 
 
The San Francisco Police Department ("SFPD") received your request, dated September 01, 2025 and given the reference number P221770-090125 for tracking purposes. 
 
Record(s) Requested: To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any extant files regarding the 1973-74 Zebra Killings, including, but not limited to, case files, audio recordings, transcripts, and inter-office memoranda regarding the arrest and conviction of four men responsible for the murder and attempted murder of nearly two dozen San Franciscans from October 1973 - April 1974.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

Upload documents directly: https://accounts.muckrock.com/accounts/login/?next=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.muckrock.com%2Faccounts%2Flogin%2F%3Fnext%3D%252Faccounts%252Fagency_login%252Fsan-francisco-police-department-367%252Fthe-zebradeath-angels-files-san-francisco-police-department-192025%252F%253Femail%253Dsanfranciscopd%252540govqa.us&url_auth_token=AAAIhg4dyZDWCRhWrg_8-S94ZA8%3A1ut0yI%3A_fXq79HDLS6md8Du2nAQvkLHBzXfR187golow0y2-IA 
 
Your request is being forwarded to the appropriate department(s) for processing and you will be notified once the request is complete. 
 
You can monitor request progress at the link below.  Thank you for using the San Francisco Public Records Center. 
 
 
San Francisco Police Department

Although SFPD has 10 calendar days to respond to your request, we are invoking an additional 14 day extension of time to respond to your request pursuant to Government Code section 7922.535 (c) (1), (2) (1) The need to search for and collect the requested records from field facilities or other establishments that are separate from the office processing the request. (2) The need to search for, collect, and appropriately examine a voluminous amount of separate and distinct records that are demanded in a single request.

Thank you for your attention. 

Sincerely, 

Yesenia Brandt 
Media Relations

Thank you for your understanding in this regard. For further information or questions, please contact Yesenia Brandt at [ASSIGNED_STAFF_EMAIL]. 
Sincerely, 
Evan Sernoffsky 
Director 
Media Relations Unit

title: Community ALPR Audit (Bloomington Police Department)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Community ALPR Audit (Bloomington Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

1. Justification Records
All records used to justify adoption of Flock Safety cameras, including but not limited to: staff reports, city council or county board memoranda, crime analysis reports, presentations, vendor proposals, and any other documents that described the purpose, anticipated benefit, or stated rationale for purchasing or leasing the system.

2. Effectiveness and Use Statistics
All statistical reports, internal summaries, use-justification documents, crime-reduction assessments, or after-action reviews evaluating the effectiveness of Flock Safety cameras since installation. This includes any records prepared in connection with contract renewals.

3. Cost and Financial Records
All financial records related to Flock Safety cameras, including invoices, purchase orders, annual subscription payments, installation costs, and maintenance costs from the date of first installation through the present. Include any records showing federal, state, grant, or civil asset forfeiture funding used to offset costs.

4. Camera Inventory and Placement
The current inventory or deployment list of Flock Safety cameras operated by or accessible to this agency, including number of units and general location descriptions. Also include any written policies, criteria, risk assessments, or internal documents used to determine camera placement locations.

5. Contracts and Legal Agreements
Copies of all active and recently expired contracts, amendments, renewals, data-sharing agreements, memoranda of understanding, and scope-of-work documents between this agency and Flock Safety.

6. Data Access and Network Sharing
(a) The Flock "Organization Audit" report showing all searches made within this agency's Flock system from the date of installation through the date of this request.
(b) The Flock "Network Audit" report showing all searches of this agency's Flock data by any outside agency from the date of installation through the date of this request.
(c) A list of all agencies currently authorized to access this agency's Flock data, including any federal agencies.
Please provide these records in their original electronic, machine-readable format (CSV or spreadsheet).

7. Security Incidents and Unauthorized Access
All records documenting any security incidents, unauthorized access, data breaches, hacking events, or unauthorized sharing of data collected by Flock Safety cameras, including any correspondence with or notifications from Flock Safety related to such incidents.

8. Secretary of State Audit
All records, correspondence, or internal communications related to the Illinois Secretary of State's 2025 audit of ALPR data-sharing practices, including any communications with the Secretary of State's office or Flock Safety regarding compliance.

9. Marketing and Sales Materials
All promotional, descriptive, and marketing materials shared with this agency by Flock Safety or any representative thereof prior to or in connection with the purchase or renewal of the Flock Safety system.

10. Communications with Flock Safety
All emails, letters, or other written communications between agency personnel and any @flocksafety.com email address from January 1, 2022, through the date this request is processed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brad Bell
